Thanks,

reply:
Dear Sarah,

Thank you for flagging this. I can confirm I see two charges on account #ACC-5892 dated June 15 and I sincerely apologize for this billing error.

I've initiated a full refund of $49 for the duplicate charge. It will appear on your statement within 5-7 business days depending on your bank.

I've also added a note to your account to ensure this doesn't recur. If you don't see the refund after 7 business days, please reply to this email and I'll escalate it immediately.

Best regards,
Hiver Support

Our finance team needs invoices for Q1 2024 (January through March) for accounting purposes. We're on the Business plan. Could you please send those over?

reply:

Let me know if your finance team needs anything else — VAT breakdown, purchase order references, or anything else.

Best,

subject: Want to upgrade from Starter to Business
incoming_email:
Hi there,

We're growing fast and our team of 3 needs to expand to around 12 users. We're currently on the Starter plan and need to move to Business. Can you walk me through the pricing and how the billing works for the switch? Do we get prorated credit for unused Starter days?

Best,
Mike Chen
reply:
Hi Mike,

Congrats on the growth — that's exciting! Here's how the upgrade works:

The Business plan is $39/user/month (billed monthly) or $29/user/month (billed annually). For 12 users that's $468/month or $348/month annually.

Yes, you'll receive a prorated credit for the remaining days on your current Starter plan, which will be applied to your first Business invoice — so you won't pay double.

To upgrade, go to Settings > Billing > Change Plan, or I can handle it from my end if you prefer. Which would you like?

Best,
Hiver Support

subject: Gmail not syncing — emails missing from shared inbox
incoming_email:
Hi,

Our shared support inbox stopped syncing with Gmail about 2 hours ago. New emails are coming into Gmail but not appearing in Hiver. Our team is working blind right now — this is urgent!

Team: support@ourcompany.com
Browser: Chrome 124
OS: macOS
reply:
Hi,

I understand how critical this is — let's get this fixed right away.

Here are the steps to try first:
1. Go to Hiver Settings > Shared Inboxes > support@ourcompany.com and click 'Re-authenticate'
2. If that doesn't work, try revoking and re-adding Hiver's Gmail permissions at myaccount.google.com > Security > Third-party apps
3. Clear Chrome cache and reload Hiver

I'm also checking our system status on my end. If none of the above resolves it within 10 minutes, please reply and I'll escalate this to our engineering team as a P1.

Hiver Support

subject: Assignment rules not triggering automatically
incoming_email:
Hello,

I set up assignment rules so that emails with the subject line containing 'refund' get automatically assigned to our billing team. But emails are still coming in unassigned. I've double checked the rule is enabled. What am I missing?

Account: hello@fashionstore.com
reply:
Hello,

Thank you for writing in. Assignment rules can be tricky — here are the most common causes:

1. **Rule order**: If another rule fires first and assigns the email, the 'refund' rule won't run. Check if a catch-all rule is above it — rules run top to bottom.
2. **Subject match**: The match is case-sensitive in some configurations. Try setting it to 'contains' (not 'equals') and check for extra spaces.
3. **Email timing**: Rules only apply to emails received after the rule was created, not retroactively.

Could you share a screenshot of your rule configuration? That'll help me spot the exact issue quickly.

Hiver Support

subject: Can't log in — getting 'session expired' error
incoming_email:
Hi,

Every time I try to log into Hiver I get a 'Your session has expired' error even though I just reset my password. I've tried Chrome, Firefox, and even incognito mode. Nothing works. I need access ASAP.

— James Carter, james@agencypartners.co
reply:
Hi James,

I'm sorry you're having trouble getting in. The 'session expired' loop after a password reset usually means there's a cached token conflict.

Please try this:
1. Clear all cookies and site data for hiver.com (not just cache)
2. Open a brand-new browser window (not incognito) and go directly to app.hiver.com
3. Use the 'Sign in with Google' button rather than email/password

If you're on a company device, your IT team may have browser policies that block third-party cookies — let me know if that's the case and I'll guide you through the admin fix.

Hiver Support

subject: Canned responses not saving
incoming_email:
Hi Hiver team,

I'm trying to create canned responses for our team but every time I click Save, the page just refreshes and the response is gone. I've tried on two different computers. Is this a known bug? We really rely on this feature.

Rachel, Operations Manager at GreenPath
reply:
Hi Rachel,

Thank you for reporting this — you're right, this is unexpected behavior and I want to get it fixed for you.

A few quick questions to help me diagnose:
1. What browser and OS are you on?
2. Does the issue happen with short canned responses too (e.g., just 'Hello, thank you!')?
3. Are you seeing any error messages in the page, or does it just silently refresh?

In the meantime, try creating the canned response from Settings > Canned Responses > New (rather than from inside a compose window) — some users have had better luck there.

I'm flagging this to our QA team as a potential bug regardless. I'll update you as soon as I have more information.

Hiver Support

subject: Hiver mobile app keeps crashing on iOS
incoming_email:
The Hiver app crashes every time I open it on my iPhone. It was working fine last week. I've tried uninstalling and reinstalling but same problem. iPhone 14 Pro, iOS 17.4.

This is really frustrating as I handle support from my phone when traveling.

- Anand
reply:
Hi Anand,

I'm sorry about the crashes — that's definitely not the experience we want for you, especially when you're traveling.

Here's what to try:
1. Go to Settings > Hiver > and toggle off/on Background App Refresh
2. Sign out of the app, force close it (swipe up), then sign back in
3. If running iOS 17.4.1 or later, please update — we pushed a patch for a crash affecting iOS 17.4

If it's still crashing after the update, please send me the approximate time of the last crash so our iOS team can pull the crash report from the backend.

Hiver Support

subject: Email notifications stopped working
incoming_email:
Hi,

I'm no longer receiving desktop notifications for new emails assigned to me. My browser notifications are enabled and I haven't changed any settings. This started 3 days ago. Other people on my team seem to be having the same issue.

Best,
Lisa from Clearwater Logistics
reply:
Hi Lisa,

Thank you for flagging this, and I'm sorry for the disruption. The fact that multiple team members are affected suggests this may be a settings-level issue rather than device-specific.

Please ask whoever manages your Hiver workspace to check: Settings > Notifications > and confirm that notifications are enabled at the workspace level (admins can override individual settings).

For your own browser: go to chrome://settings/content/notifications, find app.hiver.com, and make sure it's set to 'Allow'.

Also, we did push an update 3 days ago — I'm checking whether it may have reset notification preferences for some accounts. I'll follow up within a few hours.

Hiver Support

subject: How do I add a new team member?
incoming_email:
Hi,

We just hired a new support agent and I need to add her to our Hiver workspace. I'm the admin. What's the process? Do I need to buy an additional seat first?

Thanks,
David
reply:
Hi David,

Great — welcoming a new team member is easy in Hiver!

Yes, you'll need one additional seat. Here's the full process:
1. Go to Settings > Team Members > Invite
2. Enter her Gmail address and select her role (Agent, Manager, or Admin)
3. The system will prompt you to confirm the seat addition and update your billing prorated for the rest of the current billing cycle
4. She'll receive an invite email and can join immediately after accepting

If you're on an annual plan and prefer to manage the billing separately, just let me know and I can handle the seat addition manually.

Hiver Support

subject: Setting up a shared inbox for our new team
incoming_email:
Hello,

We just signed up for Hiver and want to set up a shared inbox for our customer support email (support@mybusiness.com). I'm not sure where to start. Could you walk us through it?

- Tom, Co-founder at NexaFlow
reply:
Hi Tom,

Welcome to Hiver — you're going to love how much smoother your team's email workflow becomes!

Here's how to set up your first shared inbox:
1. In Hiver, go to Settings > Shared Inboxes > Create New
2. Enter 'support@mybusiness.com' and authenticate it with Google (you'll need admin access to that Google account)
3. Add your team members and set their roles
4. Configure assignment rules if you want emails auto-routed

The whole process takes about 10 minutes. I'd also recommend checking out our Getting Started guide (we'll link it in your welcome email).

Feel free to reply here if you get stuck at any step — I'm happy to walk you through it.

Hiver Support

subject: Can I have multiple shared inboxes?

You can add sales@ and billing@ right away: Settings > Shared Inboxes > Create New. You can create separate assignment rules, SLA targets, and team members for each inbox, which makes it easy to keep the teams focused.

Many of our customers run 5-10 shared inboxes across departments. If you want, I can share some best-practice templates for structuring multi-inbox workflows.

Hiver Support

subject: Slack integration — any plans?
incoming_email:

My team uses Slack heavily. Would love to get Hiver notifications in Slack when a new email is assigned to me or a conversation is updated. Is this on the roadmap? Any workarounds in the meantime?

subject: Better analytics — team performance reports
incoming_email:

I manage a support team of 20 and I need better reporting. Specifically: average first response time per agent, resolution rate by category, and email volume trends by day/week. Does Hiver support this or is it coming?

Regards,
Tara Wilson, Support Director
reply:
Hi Tara,

Thank you for the detailed feedback — this is exactly the kind of input that shapes our roadmap.

Hiver's current analytics (Reports tab) covers email volume, response time averages, and resolution rates at the team level. Per-agent breakdowns and category-level filtering are available on our Elite plan if you're not already on it.

The day/week volume trend chart is available now: Reports > Volume > Group by Day.

For the per-agent first response time breakdown specifically — that's a feature we're actively working on. I'll add your vote and your use case to the product team's tracker. Would a bi-weekly CSV export of raw data work as a stopgap until the dashboard feature ships?

Hiver Support

subject: API access for custom integrations
incoming_email:
Hi Hiver,

We're a developer team and we'd like to integrate Hiver with our internal CRM. Is there a public REST API? We need to be able to create tickets, update statuses, and pull conversation data programmatically.

Thanks,
Alex (Dev Lead)
reply:
Hi Alex,

Yes — Hiver has a REST API that covers conversations, assignments, notes, and status updates. API access is available on Business and Enterprise plans.

You can find the full API documentation at developers.hiver.com, including authentication (API key-based), rate limits, and endpoint references. We also have a Postman collection you can fork to get started quickly.

For pulling conversation data programmatically, the /conversations endpoint supports filtering by inbox, date range, assignee, and status.

If you run into anything during integration, our developer support channel (in the docs) is pretty responsive. Happy to connect you with our solutions engineering team if you need a deeper walkthrough.

Hiver Support

subject: Does Hiver work with Outlook / Microsoft 365?
incoming_email:
Hi,

We're a Microsoft shop — everyone uses Outlook and Microsoft 365. We've heard great things about Hiver but it looks like it's built for Gmail. Is there any Outlook support or is it Gmail-only?

Thanks,
Caroline
reply:
Hi Caroline,

Great question — Hiver is currently built specifically for Gmail and Google Workspace. It works as a Chrome extension layered on top of Gmail, which means it requires a Gmail or Google Workspace account to function.

If your team is on Microsoft 365 with Outlook, Hiver wouldn't be a direct fit at the moment. We do get this request often, and Microsoft 365 support is something we're evaluating for the future, but I don't want to over-promise on timing.

If you're open to moving to Google Workspace, many companies make that switch specifically to use tools like Hiver — and the migration is more straightforward than it used to be. Happy to share resources if that's a path worth exploring.

Hiver Support

subject: How many users can I add to Hiver?
incoming_email:
Hello,

We're planning to roll out Hiver across our whole company. Around 80 people total, but maybe 30 will actively use the shared inboxes. Do we pay for all 80 or just the active ones? What counts as a 'user'?

— Ben
reply:
Hi Ben,

Good news — you only pay for users who are added to the Hiver workspace, not everyone in your company.

A 'user' in Hiver is anyone who has been invited and accepted a seat in your workspace. If only 30 people will actively use shared inboxes, you would add those 30 as users and pay for 30 seats. The other 50 wouldn't need to be added at all.

You can add or remove users at any time, and billing adjusts prorated. There's no penalty for scaling up or down.

Would you like me to put together a quote for 30 seats on the Business plan, including any available volume discounts?

Hiver Support

subject: GDPR compliance and data handling
incoming_email:
Hi,

We're based in the EU and our legal team requires confirmation that any tools we use are GDPR compliant. Specifically: where is our data stored, how long is it retained, can we request deletion, and do you have a DPA we can sign?

Thank you,
Legal Team, EuroRetail GmbH
reply:
Dear Legal Team,

Thank you for conducting this due diligence — these are exactly the right questions to ask.

Here are the key data handling details for Hiver:
- **Data storage**: Customer data is stored on Google Cloud infrastructure in the US by default; EU data residency is available on our Enterprise plan
- **Retention**: Email data is retained for as long as your account is active plus 30 days post-cancellation, after which it is deleted
- **Right to deletion**: Yes — you can request full data deletion at any time by submitting a request to privacy@hiver.com; we fulfil these within 30 days
- **DPA**: Yes, we offer a Data Processing Agreement fully aligned with GDPR Article 28. I'll attach our standard DPA to this email

Our full privacy policy and security documentation is available at hiver.com/security. Would it be helpful to schedule a call with our Data Privacy Officer for your legal team's review?

Hiver Support
